sourceMovie.title: Trolls
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Trolls (2016) stuck because it commits to its family premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Mike Mitchell's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The family and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Scooby-Doo! and the Legend of the Vampire
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Trolls clicked for you, Scooby-Doo! and the Legend of the Vampire (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and animation, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Spellbound
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Trolls clicked for you, Spellbound (2024) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and animation, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Trolls?
faq.answer: Try Scooby-Doo! and the Legend of the Vampire, Spellbound, The Boxtrolls—each shares DNA with Trolls while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Trolls?
faq.answer: Trolls (2016) is best described as Family, Animation, Fantasy, Adventure, Comedy, Music. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Trolls part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Trolls Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
